question: What is this project?
title: What is this project?
summary: SaaS Idea Manager gives one person a simple place to collect, develop, and manage software ideas.

scenes.headline: SaaS Idea Manager gives one person a simple place to collect, develop, and manage software ideas.
scenes.body: It is a small PHP application that stores SaaS product ideas in a local SQLite database. You create ideas, edit them, review them later, and keep a lightweight record of what you might build.

facts.label: Designed for one operator
facts.text: One trusted person uses the app. Collaboration and roles are outside the current design.

facts.label: Built with PHP and SQLite
facts.text: Server-rendered PHP templates and a file-based database on cPanel shared hosting.

facts.label: Intentionally small and easy to deploy
facts.text: No accounts, no uploads, and no framework stack—on purpose, not as unfinished work.

scenes.label: How an idea grows
scenes.description: A rough idea becomes a saved record, then a clearer opportunity, then a project worth building.

steps.title: A rough idea
steps.text: Something you might build someday—still fuzzy.

steps.title: A saved record
steps.text: Captured as a durable idea with title, notes, and status.

steps.title: A clearer opportunity
steps.text: Notes and status help you see whether it is worth pursuing.

steps.title: A project worth building
steps.text: The idea is ready to leave the list and become real work.
